item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have absolutely no idea what this was about but I adore it regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After digging through and finding extra easter eggs in 3713, and being profoundly creeped out by both those and this entry, I'm dying for part three over here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This thing successfully utilizes the principle of weirdness often used by old creepypastas. If you can't fully understand/analyze what's going on, you can't feel safe. But honestly, if you'll take it away from this skip, it will end up looking unremarkable and uninteresting. Part of me wishes that there would be more to it than what we have, so we would be able to understand what's going on a little better. The other part feels like if we would be able to come to the conclusion regarding this SCP, it would be a lot less effective in what it's supposed to do.

It's probably fine the way it is, but for me, there is not enough to like here. Novoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels like some random poorly-thought creepypasta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: here's some fanart. writer noticed when i posted it on discord and told me i could post it here if i wanted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… uh, did that last picture (truth) cause a serious freak out for anyone else? I have no idea why but that same part of my brain that kicks in at the sight of spiders did all sorts of spazzy things when I saw the truth photo.

Was there something there? I couldn't force myield to look directly at it nut it just looked like some outdoor place at night with a lamp in the distance

EDIT I just realized Truth was the second to last photo. The one that freaked me out was Home…

How did my brain freak out so bad that I perception filtered that entire thing?

Dear lord I clearly haven't slept enough these last few days. Maybe some fresh eyes will help me see what went all fucky in my amygdala.

To quote myself after every sexualencounter I've ever had, "This never happen's to me, I have no idea why it's doing that"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spooky. I’m not sure what’s going on but I feel like the writers did, that’s what counts in my book. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. The fact that "home.jpg" is a picture of a graveyard sent by "Me" is very creepy. It's almost suggesting that the viewer is going to die/is already dead. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fourthing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only very tangentially. I can assure you that we did not think of it when drafting this up, but we were thinking of some classic creepypasta vibe, which some of those old slenderman webseries definitely had, so I can see that there might be similarities.

(no there is no Slenderman involved in this)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick question: Do you know anything of TribeTwelve by any chance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What revelation have you stumbled across?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind enlightening us on what Kirby told you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It appears to be related to SCP 3173.

http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-3713

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clearly there's something going on here. I have some idea what it is now, after asking Kirby. Gonna come back for a reread later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't- it just looks like a blur

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has that oldschool creepypasta vibe that I absolutely love. The kind of weird, barely coherent stuff that you stumble across on the internet and makes you feel slightly unsafe for no apparent reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I was waiting for the second page to open up before I wrote this. To, you know, maintain the atmosphere of my other comment. But anyways. This was very fun. I think I speak for Varaxous as well when I say that I am very happy to have co-written an article that got Kirby to make something really creepy and cool like this, and then doubly happy that I got to collab it with them. This was fun because it is pure creep. Just unadulterated creep. Very, very fun.
I am also loving hiding secrets everywhere.
andtobequitehonestijustlovewritinglikethis
It makes me feel very clever, and I bet it makes Kirby feel clever too.
Plus the emoji part felt great. Anyways, I just wanted to gush about how much fun this was. Dark's word of the day: FUN!

Oh, and I made some art!
Oddly shaped phone, sure, but I'm really proud of the cracks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh this feels good. I love this kind of SCP. A++

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there doesn't appear to be a bigger message here and I don't care for "nebulous for the sake of nebulous", and I didn't personally find it that unnerving, so this is a -1 for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: also the computer seems to be owned by a person named "Jake", though that might just be the author lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like it (the images are quite neat), but it really doesn't feel like it goes anywhere. I feel like there's just not quite enough info for me to really grab onto this one. It's interesting, but no vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Christ above these kind of scps all ways freak the hell out of me, In a good way, of course.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP feels like the part two of a three parter

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kept waiting to see a man in an owl mask in one of the photos. I am thoroughly creeped out.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully creepy and unnerving. I particularly like the fact that they don't have records of why they have it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a notice, if you zoom into the laptop in truth.jpg, you can see the images name on the screen, instead of notaexit, is "getmeoutofhere" and the search bar has the words "whatisinfrontofme".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm almost unsure that this is anomalous. Until the final logs, about it receiving texts without wireless connection, nothing seems off about it. I don't dislike it, but it's a tad too nebulous to put anything together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting to note is that the file name for the main picture is Vessel.

Anyway +1 this is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know nothing that’s going on other then the fact it’s really upsetting in the good way. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's obvious enough to tell that this has some sort of connection with SCP-3713 but I'm too dumb to figure out anything beyond that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do I know what's going on? No. Was this unsettling enough that I enjoyed it without needing to know what happened? Yes.

+1

item.title: Behind Me
item.content:encoded:
itswrittenonthewalls

thinkinsidethebox

item.title: Me
item.content:encoded:
Honestly… this skip just kind of happened. It feels so strange and impromptu. It doesn't feel like something I came up with, but something that called out to me instead. Luckily I got some help from DarkStuff in making it really shine. 10/10 would collab with again.
So yeah! Thanks to Tanhony, 9Volt, MaliceAF, Varaxous, ClockworkKitten, Glewmie, CadaverCommander,
and weryllium for reviewing!

Also image credits:
Pic of SCP-3633 - https://www.flickr.com/photos/36946290@N04/9425419527
home.jpg - https://www.flickr.com/photos/134810115@N04/23861432960

All other pictures were taken by Me.

Do you understand Me?
